docs.text:
Personal Summary


I am a researcher 
with an expertise in inverse problems and 
signal processing relevant to wave propagation and 
scattering. I have developed a passion to solve real-life challenges pertaining to seismic, radar and medical imaging. 
Currently working as a 
postdoctoral associate in the 

Imaging and Computing Group
 and 
Earth Resources Laboratory
, Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).




| 
Google Scholar Citations
 (for citation statistics) 
| 
Research Gate
 (for full text of publications) 
| 
GitHub
 (software)
| 
ORCID

| 
Linkedin

| 
Earth Resources Laboratory Homepage




Current Research Interests




wave-equation based imaging and inversion


signal processing, blind deconvolution and source separation


seismic interferometry and its applications in both exploration and global seismology


parallelization and developing efficient algorithms for high performance computing in geophysical applications




Contact


Building 54-319
Dept. of Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences
77 Massachusetts Ave., Cambridge, MA 02139
E-mail address: pawbz[at]mit[dot]edu

docs.title: PhD Thesis

docs.text:
Research Experience


Focused Blind Deconvolution (FBD)




Developed this blind deconvolution method that 
extracts sparse and front-loaded impulse responses from the channel outputs, i.e., 
their convolutions with a single arbitrary source. 
[paper]


This method resolves the indeterminacy inherent to multichannel blind deconvolution and 
I have demonstrated its applications in both exploration and global seismology.


In the seismic context, the extracted impulse responses correspond to the 
Green's function of the subsurface and can be directly input to the imaging algorithms.




Blind Source Separation/ Deblending




Demonstrated the application of 
frequency-domain independent component analysis
to deblend random seismic sources. 
[slides]
 
[paper]


ICA uses higher-order statistics to measure the Gaussianity of signals and working with convolutive mixtures 
(as in seismic exploration) is a challenging problem.




Full Waveform Inversion (FWI)




I have formulated an auxiliary bump functional for seismic inversion. 
[slides]
 
[paper]


In simple words: "When the seismic waveforms are too complicated to be analyzed during inversion, a simplification of them into envelope-like bumpy waveforms can be useful."


The bump functional can be used to overcome cycle-skipping in FWI if used in a multi-objective inversion strategy.


Identified various ambiguities while parameterizing the 
        multi-parameter inverse problem for quantitative seismic imaging.

[slides]
 

[paper]


Proposed seismic inversion by maximizing the correlation between the observed and modelled seismic data. 
[slides]




Seismic Interferometry




Developed the theory of supervirtual refraction interferometry. 
[paper]


Supervirtual interferometry enhances the signal-to-noise ratio of the far-offset refracted seismic energy. It can be used to detect the presence of diving waves in the first arrivals.


Enhanced core-diffracted arrivals in the earthquake records using the theory of supervirtual interferometry. 
[slides]

docs.text:
I mainly write software in 
FORTRAN
 and 
Julia
. 
While programming in Julia, I find this 
page
 really useful. 
I have shared most 
of my contribution on 
GitHub
.
I plan to share a few other 
useful subroutines here. 


Seismic Inversion Toolbox in Julia




Documentation


A simple Jupyter 
notebook
 that demonstrates 
full waveform inversion of seismic data.




Programming Utils




A Makefile to Automatically Compile f90 Source Files with Dependencies


A 
guide
 to finalize your julia package.




Signal Processing




Construct a Minimum Phase Butter-Worth Filter in Using Log Spectra




Julia Tips




use copy! whenever possible

=
randn
(
1000
,
1000
)

=

(
1000
,
1000

a
